title: Question: How to access parameters from SC_CTHREAD
body:
---


Author Name: **Jonathon Donaldson**
Original Redmine Message: 1488 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I have a SystemC+Verilated_SV design with the following hierarchical structure:
```

SC_MODULE (TESTBENCH)
 |
 +->SC_MODULE (dut_stimulus)
 |   |
 |   +->SC_CTHREAD (my_thread1)
 |   +->SC_CTHREAD (my_thread2)
 |   +->SC_CTHREAD (my_thread3)
 |
 +->SC_MODULE (Vmy_design)

```

The my_design module also has a systemVerilog package called my_design_types. It contains a bunch of parameters, all of which are marked with /*verilator public*/.  These parameters show up just as expected in the Vmy_design_My_Design_Types.h file that is generated by verilator.

Within my top-level SystemC file (i.e. where SC_MODULE (TESTBENCH) is declared) I can then do something like this:
```
#include "Vmy_design_My_Design_Types.h"
...
Vmy_design* my_design = new Vmy_design ("My_Design");
printf ("V_MY_PARAM = %d\n", my_design->My_Design_Types->V_MY_PARAM);

```

The above works very well. But I don't actually need access to the parameters from within SC_MODULE (TESTBENCH). I actually want access to those parameters way down in SC_CTHREAD (my_thread1). Is there a best-practices/suggested way to do this?

My C++ is a bit rusty so maybe there is some absurdly obvious way to do this and I just don't remember anymore. Haha. I have one way that works already, which is to create some integer member variables within the SC_MODULE (dut_stimulus) declaration and then assign those member variables with the parameter values after I call 'new dut_stimulus' inside of SC_MODULE (TESTBENCH). But I'm not sure if there is an much simpler/better way to do it...?
code_blocks:
SC_MODULE (TESTBENCH)
 |
 +->SC_MODULE (dut_stimulus)
 |   |
 |   +->SC_CTHREAD (my_thread1)
 |   +->SC_CTHREAD (my_thread2)
 |   +->SC_CTHREAD (my_thread3)
 |
 +->SC_MODULE (Vmy_design)

#include "Vmy_design_My_Design_Types.h"
...
Vmy_design* my_design = new Vmy_design ("My_Design");
printf ("V_MY_PARAM = %d\n", my_design->My_Design_Types->V_MY_PARAM);
